PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jan-06-2016 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Dec-09-2015 Continued To Jan-06-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Franchesca Callejo for petitioner Gladys Sandin.

Ms. Callejo stated that she had contacted Mr. Morgan, the sole intestate beneficiary of the lost will, and provided the Court with a declaration by him regarding the lost will. She reported that she had spoken with an attorney, Mr. Riordan, who has a copy of the will in his files but not the original will. She contended that the fact that the intestate heir was to sole beneficiary of the lost will argues against any presumption that the will ws intentionally destroyed. She stated that if the lost will was not admitted, it would cause delay in requiring the filing of a new petition, with accompanying notice and publication requirements. The Court responded that determination whether there was sufficient evidence to overcome the rebuttable presumption that the lost will had been destroyed could only be made based upon the actual evidence before the Court. Ms. Callejo requested a continuance to discuss with her client the filing of an intestate petition. The matter was continued to January 6, 2016 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Nov-25-2015 Continued To Dec-09-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Oct-14-2015 Continued To Nov-25-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Francesca Callejo for and with (petitioner) Gladys Sandlin.

The Court stated that this is for probate of a lost will, that we have a copy but no original will and asked what evidence is available against presumption of revocation. Ms. Callejo stated that the decedent intended the will to be in effect, that the only beneficiary is her grandson, that it appears the will was prepared by an attorney who is since deceased, that the outcome of distribution will be the same as intestacy, that the decedent never had a falling out with either her sister or grandson and that they submit that this is consistent with her wishes. The Court asked if the decedent was still competent at the time of death. Ms. Callejo stated that it was doubtful, that she was conserved since 2009, that there is a petition to terminate the conservatorship pending, that it was almost impossible to communicate with her, that she was in a care facility, that her home had been sold and that neither Ms. Sandlin or Thomas Lucas had the original will. The Court stated that it will continue the matter for Ms. Callejo to be able to file a declaration regarding evidence against presumption of revocation. Ms. Callejo stated that she will submit declarations from anyone who can weigh in on this. The petition for probate of lost will and for letters testamentary was continued to November 25, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)
